title: Nonconsuming transfer violates conservation-of-value postcondition
source_title: DAML Integration Pattern - Interface Postcondition Compliance
description: A Holding template's Transfer choice creates a new holding for the receiver but fails to archive the original holding. If the choice is accidentally marked nonconsuming (or uses create without proper self-consumption), both the old and new holdings coexist, violating the conservation-of-value invariant and enabling double-spend.
recommendation: Ensure transfer choices are consuming (the DAML default). If a nonconsuming choice pattern is used, explicitly archive the current contract in the choice body before creating the replacement.

oracle.description: Transfer operations must archive the source and create the destination, maintaining conservation of value.
oracle.static_signal: Transfer-like choice that creates new contract without consuming or archiving the source contract
oracle.symbolic_property: forall h. pre(active(h)) && executes(Transfer, h) => post(not(active(h))) && exists h'. active(h') && h'.amount == h.amount

notes: Double-spend via nonconsuming transfer is the DAML equivalent of EVM's token duplication bug. Critically important for financial contracts.
